'''Jarrod O'Connor''' (born 20 July 2001) is a professional [[rugby league]] [[footballer]] who plays as a {{rlp|hk}} and {{rlp|lf}}  for the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[Super League]].

He has spent time on loan from [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] at the [[Bradford Bulls]] and [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Background==
O'Connor played his amateur rugby league with the Halton Farnworth Hornets. Jarrod is the son of former [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]], [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]], [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]], and [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] {{rlp|pr}}, [[Terry O'Connor (rugby league)|Terry O'Connor]], who now works for [[Sky Sports]].

==Career==
===Leeds Rhinos===
O'Connor made his Super League debut in round 14 of the [[Super League XXV results|2020 Super League season]] for Leeds against the [[Catalans Dragons]], he converted a [[Rhys Evans (rugby league)|Rhys Evans]] try in the 29th minute.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/leeds-vs-catalans/teams/54288|title=Starting Lineups - Leeds vs Catalans|publisher=Sky Sports |date=30 September 2020}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/1587/round-14-fixtures-confirmed|title=Round 14 fixtures confirmed|publisher=Sky Sports |date=29 September 2020}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leeds-rhinos-6-catalans-dragons-34-youthful-rhinos-impress-spirited-display-against-dragons-2988868|title=Leeds Rhinos 6 Catalans Dragons 34: Youthful Rhinos impress in spirited display against Dragons |publisher=Yorkshire Evening Post |first=Peter |last=Smith |date=30 September 2020}}</ref>
On 24 September 2022, O'Connor played for Leeds in their 24–12 loss to [[St Helens RFC]] in the [[2022 Super League Grand Final]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2022/sep/24/st-helens-leeds-super-league-grand-final-match-report-rugby-league/|title=St Helens sink Leeds to win record fourth consecutive Grand Final|newspaper=The Observer|date=24 September 2022 |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron }}</ref>
O'Connor played 27 games for Leeds in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished 8th on the table and missed the playoffs.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leeds-rhinos-2023-season-review-picking-out-the-best-and-worst-players-performances-results-4355920/|title=Leeds Rhinos' 2023 season review: picking out the best and worst players, performances, results|website=www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk}}</ref>
O'Connor played 27 games for Leeds in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished 8th on the table and missed the playoffs.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/from-310-to-810-peter-smiths-leeds-rhinos-player-ratings-for-2024-season-4799226/|title=Leeds Rhinos player ratings from the 2024 Super League season|website=www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk}}</ref>

===Bradford Bulls (loan)===
On 2 December 2021, it was reported that he had signed for [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=2 Dec 2021|accessdate=2 Dec 2021|title=Leeds loan three players to Bradford|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/leeds-loan-three-players-to-bradford/}}</ref>
